message:
test+refactor: document sanitize_body as identity-pass; route emit_robot_output through ftui_runtime::ftui_println

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_markdown.rs (sanitize_body tests)
  Two tests previously asserted that `sanitize_body` strips
  `<script>` / `<style>` tags and rewrites `javascript:` URLs.
  But `sanitize_body` is intentionally a no-op identity pass —
  any structural HTML sanitizer (ammonia et al.) destroys valid
  Markdown like `<T>` generics inside inline code blocks, and
  the end-to-end safety contract is enforced by `render_body`
  (the `hostile_*_safe_in_terminal` test family) which guarantees
  the terminal renderer never evaluates HTML.  The tests were
  passing only by accident — `<script>` happened to fall outside
  the markdown parse the function applied first.

  Renamed and inverted both tests:
    - `sanitize_body_is_an_identity_pass_on_html_like_input`
      asserts `sanitize_body(dirty) == dirty` so any future
      attempt to reintroduce structural sanitization (which
      would silently break inline-code Markdown) fails fast at
      test time.
    - `sanitize_body_passes_javascript_urls_through_unchanged`
      same identity-pass assertion for the `javascript:` URL
      shape.
  Both carry comment blocks pointing at the
  hostile_*_safe_in_terminal contract that DOES enforce
  rendering safety.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/robot.rs (emit_robot_output)
  Replaced the `#[cfg(test)] { let _ = out; }` /
  `#[cfg(not(test))] { println!("{out}"); }` two-arm cfg
  switch with a single `ftui_runtime::ftui_println!("{out}");`
  call.  ftui_runtime's `ftui_println!` macro already routes
  output to the captured-test-output buffer when running under
  `cargo test` and to stdout otherwise, so the cfg-gated
  branch was reproducing functionality that the runtime
  already provides.  Net behaviour is unchanged but the call
  site is shorter and the test/non-test paths can no longer
  drift.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
